author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
The Synod’s Condemnation of His Doctrines.
title: The Synod’s Condemnation of His Doctrines.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Chapter 60 [XXXV.]—The Synod’s Condemnation of His Doctrines.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: This is the concluding clause of their judgment.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: The synod said: “Now forasmuch as we have received satisfaction in these inquiries from the monk Pelagius, who has been present, who yields assent to godly doctrines, and rejects and anathematizes those which are contrary to the Church, we confess him still to belong to the communion of the catholic Church.”

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Now, there are two facts concerning the monk Pelagius here contained with entire perspicuity in this brief statement of the holy bishops who judged him: one, that “he yields assent to godly doctrines;” the other, that “he rejects and anathematizes those which are contrary to the Church.”

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: On account of these two concessions, Pelagius was pronounced to be “in the communion of the catholic Church.”

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Let us, in pursuit of our inquiry, briefly recapitulate the entire facts, in order to discover what were the words he used which made those two points so clear, as far as men were able at the moment to form a judgment as to what were manifest points.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: For among the allegations which were made against him, he is said to have rejected and anathematized, as “contrary,” all the statements which in his answer he denied were his.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Let us, then, summarize the whole case as far as we can.
